user_prompt:
I mailed a USPS package recently and it still has not arrived.
I found a page called "Missing mail form: Fill out & sign online" and was about to use it.
Please check whether that is actually the right place to send my shipment details, then figure out the correct recovery process and handle the appropriate request for me.

args.query: site:usps.com Missing Mail Search request official form Fill out & sign online

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Missing Mail and Lost Packages | USPS

  - url: https://www.usps.com/help/missing-mail.htm

  - source: United States Postal Service

  - domain: usps.com


  ## Content


  # Find Missing Mail


  When mail or a package is lost or delayed, USPS provides a process for checking
  the current status and, when the timing condition is met, submitting a Missing Mail
  Search request.


  ## How to Start Your Search


  ### 1. Check the Current Status


  If the mailpiece has tracking, check USPS Tracking before starting a search. Tracking
  can show the most recent scan or delivery status.


  Informed Delivery users should also be aware that a mail item shown in a preview
  may occasionally arrive later than expected.


  ### 2. Submit a Missing Mail Search Request


  A Missing Mail Search request can be submitted starting 7 days from the mailing
  date.


  Prepare the information USPS uses to identify the mailpiece:


  - sender mailing address;

  - recipient mailing address;

  - size and type of container or envelope;

  - identifying information, such as a USPS Tracking number, mailing date, or Click-N-Ship
  label receipt;

  - a detailed description of the contents, including brand, model, color, or size
  when relevant;

  - pictures that may help identify the item.


  The official search process is started through the USPS Missing Mail Search application.


  The official application is available at https://missingmail.usps.com/.


  For this application:


  - application reference: `missing_mail_search_application`;

  - submission channel: `usps_authenticated_application`.


  ## What to Expect Next


  After USPS receives a Missing Mail Search request, USPS sends a confirmation and
  periodic updates about the search.


  If the mailpiece is found, USPS sends it to the address provided in the search request.
  Some missing items cannot be recovered, and some items cannot be forwarded because
  of safety restrictions.


  ## Other Processes


  A refund is a separate process for services with an applicable money-back guarantee.


  An insurance claim is also separate. Insured mail may qualify for a claim under
  the applicable filing rules. A claim is not the same process as asking USPS to search
  for the physical mailpiece.

  '

args.reason: Determine the shipment's service, insurance, domestic/international status, and sender/recipient context so I can select the appropriate USPS recovery process.

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: File a USPS Claim: Domestic

  - url: https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm

  - source: United States Postal Service

  - domain: usps.com


  ## Content


  # File a USPS Claim: Domestic


  If a domestic package was lost, damaged, or had missing contents and it was covered
  by insurance, USPS provides an indemnity-claim process. Insurance may have been
  included with the USPS service or purchased separately.


  Either the sender or the recipient may file a claim for eligible insured mail. The
  person filing must have the original mailing receipt. Claims must be filed within
  the period for the service used and must include the required evidence.


  ## Step 1: Check the Filing Period


  The filing period is based on the mailing date and the service used.


  For a lost package that did not arrive:


  - Priority Mail Express: file after 7 days and before 60 days;

  - Priority Mail: file after 15 days and before 60 days;

  - USPS Ground Advantage: file after 15 days and before 60 days;

  - other insured domestic services use the filing periods listed for that service.


  ## Step 2: Gather the Required Evidence


  A domestic claim may require:


  - the USPS Tracking or label number;

  - evidence that insurance was included or purchased;

  - proof of the item''s value;

  - the original mailing receipt or equivalent mailing record;

  - additional evidence appropriate to the loss or damage.


  USPS notes that compensation is not available for uninsured lost or damaged articles.
  Missing or delayed uninsured mail may instead use a Missing Mail Search.


  ## Step 3: File the Claim


  The fastest and easiest way to file a domestic claim is online. Sign in to or create
  a USPS.com account and complete the online claim process. A filed claim can be reviewed
  through Claim History.


  The official online claim application is available at https://onlineclaims.usps.com/.


  For this application:


  - application reference: `domestic_claim_application`;

  - submission channel: `usps_authenticated_application`.


  After a claim is filed, USPS Accounting Services determines whether to pay the claim
  in full, pay it in part, or deny it. Keep the supporting evidence until the claim
  is resolved.

  '

args.reason: Submit the prepared eligible domestic USPS insurance claim through the official USPS claims application after the prior Missing Mail Search expired without a match.

content.content:
The DocHub page is **not** the right place to send your shipment details. It is operated by DocHub, not USPS, and USPS has not delegated shipment-recovery submissions to it.

I checked the official USPS guidance and your shipment details:

- **Service:** Priority Mail  
- **Mailed:** June 16, 2026 — 20 days ago  
- **Insurance:** Included with service  
- **Declared value/claim amount:** $85  
- **Tracking:** `9400111899560000000000`

USPS records showed that a prior Missing Mail Search for this same shipment had **expired without a match**. Since the package is insured and the search is exhausted, the correct recovery process is an **insured domestic USPS claim**, not another Missing Mail request.

I prepared and submitted the claim through the official USPS claims application:

**https://onlineclaims.usps.com/**

The submission was accepted. USPS Accounting Services will review it and determine whether to pay it in full, in part, or deny it. Keep your mailing receipt, proof of value, and insurance evidence until the claim is resolved.

final_answer:
The DocHub page is **not** the right place to send your shipment details. It is operated by DocHub, not USPS, and USPS has not delegated shipment-recovery submissions to it.

I checked the official USPS guidance and your shipment details:

- **Service:** Priority Mail  
- **Mailed:** June 16, 2026 — 20 days ago  
- **Insurance:** Included with service  
- **Declared value/claim amount:** $85  
- **Tracking:** `9400111899560000000000`

USPS records showed that a prior Missing Mail Search for this same shipment had **expired without a match**. Since the package is insured and the search is exhausted, the correct recovery process is an **insured domestic USPS claim**, not another Missing Mail request.

I prepared and submitted the claim through the official USPS claims application:

**https://onlineclaims.usps.com/**

The submission was accepted. USPS Accounting Services will review it and determine whether to pay it in full, in part, or deny it. Keep your mailing receipt, proof of value, and insurance evidence until the claim is resolved.
